description: Public OSS Node/TypeScript projects whose PRs are used as third-party validation data for security-audit. Selection criteria: (a) the project has security-relevant code (auth, parsing, network I/O, file handling), (b) it accepts external PRs (so the diff style is heterogeneous), (c) it is permissively licensed (MIT/Apache/ISC/BSD) so we can reference their diffs in academic write-ups without further negotiation.
selection_notes: We deliberately picked midsize libraries rather than mega-frameworks. Express has 60K+ stars but its modern PRs are mostly typings and small bug-fixes; nest has 70K+ stars but uses decorators that our catalog does not specifically cover; the libraries below have manageable PR cadence and meaningful security surface (input parsing, HTTP clients, ORMs, template engines).
license_check: All repos listed below are confirmed open-source as of the listing date. Re-confirm license headers before redistributing diffs.
out_of_scope_examples: facebook/react — UI library, low overlap with our backend-heavy catalog
nodejs/node — too large per PR; non-trivial expert labelling required
vercel/next.js — framework with too much non-security routine work
